assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.
The intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognised monetary receivable/payable position.
The issue is the possible designation of foreign currency risk in a hedging relationship.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk, including certain forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 lists hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model relevant to foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.
